steps.title: Read circle P from standard form
steps.content: Use $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$ to read circle P's center and radius, then apply the stated shift and diameter change to circle Q.

steps.title: Identify center and radius of circle P
steps.content: Standard form $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$ has center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$. For $(x+4)^2 + (y-6)^2 = 16$: center $(-4, 6)$, radius $\sqrt{16} = 4$.

steps.title: Find radius of circle Q
steps.content: Diameter of Q is double that of P. P has diameter $8$, so Q has diameter $16$ and radius $8$. Thus $r^2 = 64$.

steps.title: Write the final equation
steps.content: Circle Q has center $(-2,3)$ and radius $8$, so its equation is $$(x+2)^2+(y-3)^2=64.$$ That matches D.
